title: euodia quercifolia
intro: Euodia quercifolia, also known as Chinese rowan or rowan chrysomelid, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to China. It belongs to the Rutaceae family and has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties. The bark, leaves, and roots of the Euodia quercifolia plant are used in herbal preparations, which are valued for their anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, providing health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting digestion. Euodia quercifolia is often used in teas, decoctions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and gastrointestinal problems.
